author: Wei Wang
message:
Merge pull request #2554 from onevcat/fix/swiftui-context-sharing-2551

Fix SwiftUI image context sharing between copied image views

author: onevcat
message:
Harden SwiftUI context copy-on-mutate

- Route the deprecated `onFailureImage(_:)` on `KFImageProtocol` through
  `copyForMutation()`; it still mutated the shared context.
- Unify all SwiftUI modifiers on `copyForMutation()` instead of
  duplicating the copy boilerplate at each call site.
- Make `Context.copy()` take an atomic snapshot in a single queue sync,
  avoiding per-property queue hops and value tearing.
- Document `copyForMutation()` and `Context.copy()`, including the
  reminder to keep `copy()` in sync with new stored properties.
- Add regression tests: modifier chains preserve earlier settings
  (guards against missed fields in `copy()`), and `onFailureImage`
  no longer mutates the original view value.

author: onevcat
message:
Add disk storage tests for auto ext file name validation

Pin the storage-layer behavior introduced in #2540 (fix for #2301):

- A key whose URL path contains '@' produces a plain hashed file name
  with no extension, and the store/retrieve roundtrip works.
- A key with a query string falls back to an extension-less hashed
  file name instead of a raw query-carrying one.

Avoid full-size Data copies on internal download data reads

author: Wei Wang
message:
Merge pull request #2539 from devzahirul/fix/force-cancel-callbacks-race

Fix data race in SessionDataTask.forceCancel()

author: onevcat
message:
Avoid full-size Data copies on internal download data reads

The defensive copy introduced in #2524 (Data(_mutableData) in the
mutableData getter) allocates a buffer as large as the downloaded data
on every completion, and once per received chunk for progressive
decoding. On memory-constrained devices that allocation can fail, and
Foundation traps (EXC_BREAKPOINT in __DataStorage) instead of throwing,
which is the crash reported in #2543.

Add an internal zero-copy accessor that returns the accumulated data as
a COW share and use it on the internal paths (download completion and
progressive decoding). The public mutableData getter keeps its snapshot
semantics. Sharing is safe: later appends copy on write and never mutate
storage seen by previously returned values.

author: zahirulAIIUB
message:
Fix broken disk caching when the image URL path contains '@'

With `autoExtAfterHashedFileName` enabled, the cache file extension is derived
from the key by splitting on `@` (to drop the processor identifier) and taking
the text after the last `.`. When the URL path itself contains `@`, e.g.
`https://t.furaffinity.net/57373197@300-1720981878.jpg`, the split yields
`https://t.furaffinity.net/57373197` and the extracted extension becomes
`net/57373197`. The path separator turns the hashed cache file name into a
nonexistent sub-directory path, so the file is never written and disk caching
silently breaks.

Only accept a plausible file extension (non-empty, short, alphanumeric);
otherwise fall back to no extension. Add unit tests covering the regression.

Fixes #2301

author: zahirulAIIUB
message:
Fix data race in SessionDataTask.forceCancel()

`forceCancel()` iterated `callbacksStore.keys` without holding `lock`, while the session delegate queue mutates the store via `addCallback`/`completeAndRemoveAllCallbacks`. That is a read/write data race on a Swift `Dictionary`, reachable from the public `ImageDownloader.cancelAll()` and `cancel(url:)` on arbitrary caller threads. It also mutated the dictionary mid-iteration via `cancel(token:)` -> `removeCallback`.

Snapshot the tokens under the lock, then cancel outside it (the non-recursive lock would otherwise deadlock). Add a regression test that reproduces the race under Thread Sanitizer: it fails with a 'Swift access race' and crash before the fix, and passes cleanly after.

author: onevcat
message:
Fix runner labels and adjust CI matrix

macos-26 is already ARM64; macos-26-arm64 is not a valid label.
Move 26.0.1 to build-only (no simulator runtimes available),
use 26.2 as oldest test version (has pre-installed simulators).

Final matrix:
- test: 26.2, 26.5
- build: 26.0.1, 26.3, 26.4.1

author: onevcat
message:
Switch CI runners to macos-26-arm64 and fix simulator setup

- Use macos-26-arm64 (Apple Silicon) for all CI jobs
- Add downloadPlatform step for Xcode 26.0.1 iOS/tvOS tests
  since the runner doesn't have 26.0 simulator runtimes pre-installed

author: onevcat
message:
Fix Xcode 26.0.1 test destinations to use available simulators

The macos-26 runner only has simulator runtimes 26.2, 26.4, and 26.5
pre-installed. Use OS 26.2 for Xcode 26.0.1 iOS/tvOS tests.

author: onevcat
message:
Update CI matrix to Xcode 26 only

Drop all Xcode 16.x entries since App Store Connect now requires
Xcode 26+. New matrix:
- test: Xcode 26.0.1, 26.5
- build: Xcode 26.2, 26.3, 26.4.1
